origin.name: Singapore
origin.investigating_authority: Singapore Police Force and Health Sciences Authority
origin.post_mortem_trigger: Unexpected, unnatural, or suspicious deaths referred to coroner
origin.source: FCDO Singapore travel advice, updated 2025; British High Commission Singapore guidance

destinations.name: United Kingdom
special_rules: Documentation is in English throughout. No translation required.
Singapore has an efficient civil registration system. Documentation is typically issued promptly.
Excellent direct flight connections to Heathrow via Singapore Airlines.
destinations.dest_reception: The UK funeral director takes custody at the cargo terminal. Singapore documentation is in English and typically accepted directly. The coroner for the district is notified.
destinations.dest_consular_note: FCDO 24-hour emergency line: +44 (0)20 7008 5000. The British High Commission in Singapore can register the death and advise.
destinations.seo_title: Singapore to UK Repatriation: Guidance for British Families
destinations.seo_description: Someone has died in Singapore. Repatriation to the UK takes 7-14 days. Efficient documentation and direct Singapore Airlines flights. Contact us 24/7.

destinations.name: Ireland

airlines.name: Singapore Airlines
airlines.routes: SIN to DUB (via LHR or AMS)

special_rules: Irish Embassy in Singapore handles consular matters.
Documentation in English. No translation required.
destinations.dest_reception: The Irish funeral director takes custody at the cargo terminal. Singapore documentation in English. The Coroner is notified.
destinations.dest_consular_note: Department of Foreign Affairs emergency line: +353 1 408 2000. The Irish Embassy in Singapore can register the death and advise.
destinations.seo_title: Singapore to Ireland: Funeral Repatriation Guidance
destinations.seo_description: Someone has died in Singapore and needs to come home to Ireland. 7-14 days. Documentation in English and direct flights. Contact us 24/7.
